description: Explicit registry of tables exempt from PostgreSQL Row-Level Security. Per Stage 04 §20, CI fails if a new table appears without classification.

exceptions.reason: Root table of the multi-tenant model. Cannot have a tenant_id column because it IS the tenant. Access is controlled by membership validation in AuthService (a user can only authenticate against a tenant they have a membership in).
exceptions.readAuthorization: Authenticated user with an active membership in the tenant. AuthService validates membership at login time.
exceptions.writeAuthorization: Platform admin only (no public API for tenant CRUD). Seeded via Flyway migrations or platform admin tooling.
exceptions.dataSensitivity: LOW — tenant name and subdomain are not secret. Status is operational metadata.
exceptions.justification: Cannot apply RLS on the tenants table itself — it has no tenant_id column. Defense-in-depth is provided by: (1) AuthService membership validation at login, (2) JwtAuthenticationFilter tenant binding, (3) TenantContextFilter establishing verified context.

exceptions.reason: Platform-wide catalog of RBAC capabilities (e.g. USER.CREATE, ORGANIZATION.READ). The same set of capabilities is available to all tenants. There is no tenant-specific capability set.
exceptions.readAuthorization: Any authenticated user may read the capability catalog (CAPABILITY.READ).
exceptions.writeAuthorization: CAPABILITY.MANAGE (platform admin). No tenant-scoped write — capabilities are global.
exceptions.dataSensitivity: NONE — capability codes and names are public platform metadata.
exceptions.justification: Intentionally global reference data. Applying RLS would require every tenant to maintain its own capability catalog, which contradicts the platform's RBAC model. The catalog is seeded by V14/V15 migrations and is read-only for tenants.
